title: Fixation of Aiptasia larvae for observation of native fluorescence of fluorescent proteins
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"116m9","text":"This protocol is for fixation of Aiptasia (Exaiptasia diaphana) larvae which express fluorescent proteins (tested with mNeonGreen, mScarlet-I and mTurquoise2) to preserve their native fluorescence. The resulting samples can be imaged under a widefield fluorescence microscope with sufficient image quality. For highest quality images, a confocal laser-scanning microscope can be used. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":43,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ugfd","text":"It is recommended to do all the pipetting under a stereo-microscope with a wide field of view.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"931kk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5tm04","text":"This protocol highly benefits from well maintained and greased pipettes (making accidental sucking up of larvae less likely and being easier on your thumb if processing lots of samples).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d3oti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72tu5","text":"Always keep living larvae submerged in liquid, they will immediately disintegrate otherwise.  Besides that living larvae older than 1 d are very robust and can be pipetted without caution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2chmb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ivfs","text":"Using new polystyrene plates is recommended because of their hydrophobicity so ASW forms nice round drops and larvae get pulled together by the surface tension when removing liquid. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7qfd6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5gbq2","text":"Fixed larvae are pretty fragile when it comes to pipetting, try to only remove liquid during the fixation and washing steps and not to suck in larvae, the shear forces can rupture/damage them or remove the apical tuft. This is less of an issue once they are in viscous mounting medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vnh2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2r680","text":"If larvae get stuck on the polystyrene, try to loosen them with a gentle stream from the pipette. Scraping them off or trying to suck them into the tip will not work or damage them.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3esde","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aqmt9","text":"If you are new to handling larvae, get some practice first and do some test fixations and washes with dispensable plain larvae to get a good feeling how they behave (alive as well as fixed).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":190}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1sp76","text":"Place PBST in a 50-mL conical-bottom tube on ice or at 4°C ≥1 h before starting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"aao47","text":"Safety Remarks:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7uct5","text":"Be sure to follow chemical safety procedures. Formaldehyde is carcinogenic, acutely toxic by skin contact, inhalation, or ingestion. It is also flammable and toxic to aquatic organisms and can cause serious\neye damage or even blindness on eye contact. Read the safety data sheet, wear nitrile gloves (do not re-use them), safety glasses and a lab coat. Dispose liquid waste containing the fixatives in a dedicated tightly closing container and dispose according to liquid chemical waste regulations. Clean up spills immediately and dispose used paper towels in accordance with solid chemical waste regulations. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":46,"length":13},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":301,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

clay","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erom2","text":"Artificial seawater (ASW) with 32-34 g/L salinity, made from Red Sea Coral Pro mL:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2365l","text":"2.5 mL of 16% Formaldehyde from Paraformaldehyde in aqueous solution (e.g. Electron mL ASW","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7iekh","text":"Store protected from light at 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e7fhg","text":"Do not use formalin or other formaldehyde solutions with methanol/alcohols as PBS:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ga3a","text":"For 1 L:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fr5h","text":"80 g NaCl","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rqno","text":"2 g KCl","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9b4j1","text":"14.4 g Na2HPO4 (di-Sodium Hydrogen Phosphate)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajumo","text":"2.4 g KH2PO4 (Potassium di-Hydrogen Phosphate)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nfic","text":"Dissolve in 800 mL of ddH2O, adjust pH to 7.4 with HCl if necessary and then add ddH2O to 1 L. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"40m11","text":"Autoclave. Store at room temperature. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"abq2h","text":"For 1× PBS add 900 mL dH2O to 100 mL 10× PBS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3inif","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"l321","text":"PBST:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gcos","text":"Add 1 mL (0.1% v/v) Tween-20 (e.g., Sigma-Aldrich P1379) to 1 L 1× PBS, mix well. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fqkj3","text":"Store at room temperature or 4°C (I usually have a 0.5-1 L bottle at room temperature and some 50 mL\naliquots at 4°C to have them already cold when I need them).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cmfds","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4k6sk","text":"4-well plates: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5m9i","text":"We are using these plates which have a decent price compared to the Nunc ones: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d4886","text":"CELLTREAT Scientific Products 4 Well Non-treated Plate, Sterile. Man. #: 229503, Fisher Scientific order #: 50-114-911 - ~$120/50 plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6s43s","text":"ALTERNATIVE: Chambered glass slides or cover slips, e.g., IBIDI 80841 or 80821 with adjustment of the liquid volumes used. This helps preventing \"loss\" of fixed larvae adhering to the surface as they don't need to be transferred during the protocol. Just make sure that the materials of the chambers are compatible with the reagents used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":338}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3utt7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajlf9","text":"DIY anti-fade mounting medium (aqueous, 90% glycerol; Kiernan et al., 1997):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":76}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"drtng","text":"For 50 mL:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3t4ug","text":"45 mL glycerol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ehjo8","text":"5 mL 10× PBS pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aiv0n","text":"250 mg N-propyl gallate (e.g., Sigma-Aldrich P3130)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4flv6","text":"Optional: 388 µL of 200 mM NaN3 as preservative and antioxidant","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alcvo","text":"Store aliquoted at −80°C, working aliquot can be stored at −20°C for weeks. Let warm to ≤ 4°C before using.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2pgjn","text":"Commercial anti-fade mounting media can also be used (at enormous costs), be sure they don't contain organic solvents and are compatible with the fluorophores used. Hard-setting media seem to severely damage the larvae. SlowFade Glass (ThermoFisher Scientific S36917) yielded the best results, other media of the SlowFade series will likely work as well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":354},{"style":"bold","offset":220,"length":47}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1n1td","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1bad","text":"Simple aqueous 90% glycerol mounting medium:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9poqi","text":"For 50 mL:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r2cg","text":"45 mL glycerol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"br8p4","text":"5 mL 10× PBS pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1fg7n","text":"Optional: 388 µL of 200 mM NaN3 as preservative and antioxidant","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1e4p5","text":"Store at 4°C to prevent microbial growth.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"46imo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85g4l","text":"VALAP for sealing cover slips (Chazotte et al., 2011):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":54}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92ea4","text":"Heat (low setting of a heat plate) equal amounts of Vaseline (petroleum jelly), Lanolin and paraffin together in a glass beaker and stir well once all components have liquified to get a uniform mixture. Aliquot into small glass jars and let solidify.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ol9e","text":"Valap is basically a wax-like substance which has the advantages over other ways of sealing (for example nail-polish) that it is hydrophobic and doesn't contain any solvents which might mix with the aqueous sample and interfere with viability and fluorescence.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":260}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"evfkm","text":"The protocol for making the Valap for sealing the sample can be found under the following link: http://cshprotocols.cshlp.org/content/2010/12/pdb.rec12380","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":96}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":96,"length":58}],"data":{}},{"key":"1miqi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"DDCA3CAECD404C6D913131BCCD4CF5AB","url":"http://cshprotocols.cshlp.org/content/2010/12/pdb.rec12380"}}}}
steps: Add 150 µL of fresh ASW into a well of a new 4-well plate per sample. Do not use cleaned, previously used plates, the larvae will stick to it and youmight lose and/or damage a lot during the fixation procedure.

Transfer larvae with a 10-µL pipet into the wells. A 20-µL pipet which uses 10-µL XL tips is even better, e.g., Eppendorf Research Plus 2 – 20 µL, light gray, 3123000098. pipets with 200-µL tips work in principal as well but are much more inefficient and transfer a lot of ASW with the larvae.
Incubate at RT for 5 min. During this time the dead larvae sink to the bottom of the well, allowing to remove excess liquid in the next step more thoroughly.
Add a roughly equal volume of Fixative 2 (Fixative 1 is not used in this protocol).
Remove some excess ASW (and any coarse dirt or lint if possible) with a 200-µL pipet.
Remove as much liquid as possible while keeping the larvae submerged.
Incubate at 4°C for 1 h with slow agitation at 50-70 rpm.
Remove excess liquid and replace it with 500 µL of Fixative 2.

Add 100 µL DIY anti-fade mounting medium or 90% glycerol in PBS (1× final strength). Volume can be increased or decreased depending on how much liquid was left in previous step. The lower the final glycerol percentage, the lower the refraction index and image quality.
Remove liquid almost completely until larvae are in a minimal amount in the corner of the well. The less liquid left here, the higher the refraction index of the sample for microscopy.

Do 5 washes with 500 µL of ice-cold PBST and gently shake at ~50-70 rpm (so the liquid and larvae are barely moving) on ice for 5 min at every step. Remove excess liquid with a 200-µL pipet tip after every wash step.
Seal with VALAP (or, less ideal, clear nail polish).

Transfer larvae in 10-20 µL liquid onto glass slide, top with coverslip and small clay feet, push carefully on the clayfeet until the distance between slide and cover slip is not much more than the larval body diameter. Larvae can be concentrated by a series of pipetting steps with successively decreasing volumes.

Incubate 1 h to overnight in the dark at 4°C with gentle agitation to help the mounting medium mix with the remaining PBST and diffuse into the larvae. Overnight incubation results in better optical quality but fluorescence signals might fade over time and will likely be best if imaging the samples the same day.

Store the slides in the dark at 4°C until imaging. The native fluorescence of the fluorescent proteins will fade over time, so it is best to image the same or next day. Storage at −20°C might extend the time but requires that the glycerol has really penetrated the whole larval bodies.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
